_comment: userPromptSubmitted hook: detects the deterministic `summarize:` prefix and emits an audit/telemetry line. Per docs (https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/hooks-configuration) `userPromptSubmitted` output is NOT honored by the runtime today ("prompt mutation not supported"), so this hook cannot itself invoke the extension or rewrite the prompt. The synergy pattern this models is: (a) hook deterministically detects the trigger, (b) operator/automation observes the log, (c) the agent — guided by the `summarize:` convention in the user prompt — calls the `clab_summarize` tool. See lab §5 for the limitation discussion.

userPromptSubmitted.bash: INPUT=$(cat); PROMPT=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.prompt // ""'); case "$PROMPT" in summarize:*) printf '[clab-auto-summarize] trigger detected at %s for cwd=%s\n' "$(date -u +%FT%TZ)" "$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.cwd // "?"')" >&2 ;; esac; exit 0
userPromptSubmitted.powershell: $raw = [Console]::In.ReadToEnd(); $evt = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json; if ($evt.prompt -match '^summarize:') { [Console]::Error.WriteLine("[clab-auto-summarize] trigger detected at $((Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o')) for cwd=$($evt.cwd)") }; exit 0
userPromptSubmitted.comment: Detects deterministic `summarize:` prefix and logs to stderr (CLI surfaces it as a hook diagnostic). Does not — and cannot — auto-invoke the extension; see lab §5 on the runtime limitation.
